message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "infra-fit" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: A new Rust binary is not justified at current scale — extend the existing setup or pilot Tart/libvirt incrementally
DETAIL: Honest assessment: the fleet is two hosts (one Mac via self-hosted-oss + Colima/launchd, one Jeff-Ubuntu via self-hosted-colima Lima VM + 16-runner compose + systemd --user). The proposal's unified-CLI value proposition targets a multi-host, multi-backend problem this repo does not yet have. The genuinely valuable ideas are (1) per-job VM isolation (would delete the disk-cleanup, cache-integrity, workdir-collision, and zombie-runner problem classes at the root — very aligned with the repo's root-cause-first principle) and (2) deduplicating the two stacks (the same pre-job-hook fix was applied twice, commits 0794779451 and the oss variant — real drift cost). But neither requires a Rust abstraction layer: (1) can be piloted by adding a couple of Tart-backed runners on the Mac alongside the existing fleet, and (2) is a bash refactor (shared hook/monitor library sourced by both stacks). The Rust skeleton adds a compile/distribute/version toolchain for ~30 lines of real logic while deferring 100% of the operationally hard code. Rust becomes justified only if/when the fleet grows to many heterogeneous hosts needing a typed state machine with tests — not before.
RECOMMENDATION: Reject gha-isolated as a rewrite. Extract its one good idea as a bounded experiment: a Tart (macOS) or libvirt (Linux) per-job-VM runner pilot managed by a new script in self-hosted-oss/, installed via install.sh, with the existing monitor/alert/cleanup mesh pointed at it. Deduplicate the two bash stacks into a shared lib as a separate PR. Revisit a compiled orchestrator only if host count grows beyond what install.sh-per-host can manage.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

Web search results for query: ""gha-outrunner" GitHub Actions runner"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner: Ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions — no Kubernetes required","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner"},{"title":"Get started with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/use-actions-runner-controller/get-started"},{"title":"GitHub - rust-lang/gha-runner: Custom fork of the GitHub Actions runner","url":"https://github.com/rust-lang/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - chaddyc/gha-runner: GitHub Actions Runner Docker Container · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/chaddyc/gha-runner"},{"title":"Deploying runner scale sets with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/how-tos/manage-runners/use-actions-runner-controller/deploy-runner-scale-sets"},{"title":"Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/actions-runner-controller"},{"title":"GitHub - omsf/gha-runner: A simple GitHub Action for creating cloud-based self-hosted runners. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/omsf/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - PasseiDireto/gh-runner: Self Hosted GitHub Actions Runner · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/PasseiDireto/gh-runner"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about "gha-outrunner":

## Overview

gha-outrunner is an ephemeral GitHub Actions runner solution that provisions fresh Docker containers or VMs for each GitHub Actions job, then destroys them when the job completes.

## Key Advantages

Unlike GitHub's Actions Runner Controller (ARC) which requires Kubernetes, gha-outrunner gives you isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart without needing an additional orchestrator.

## How It Works

It uses GitHub's scaleset API to register as an autoscaling runner group.

## Provisioning Options

The project supports multiple deployment backends:
- Containers per job with fastest startup
- KVM VMs from qcow2 golden images with CoW overlays using QEMU Guest Agent for command execution
- Tart guest agent for command execution on VM clones per job

For more details and documentation, you can visit the [official GitHub repository](https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.reasoning: Every factual claim in the finding checks out against the repo and git history. (1) Fleet is two hosts: self-hosted-oss README/launchd files = Mac Colima stack; self-hosted-colima README/install.sh = Jeff-Ubuntu with systemd --user colima-runners.service + Lima VM + 16-runner compose (org-runner-16 present in docker-compose.yml; README's '10' is stale). No GCE setup exists in either stack. (2) Duplicate pre-job-hook fix confirmed: oss disk-cleanup added in 8c34a4f1bf (PR #7770), colima copy in 0794779451 ('mirrors self-hosted-oss/ version'); diff shows near-identical logic with drift, and b2c58658cd was a third fix needed for the colima copy — real drift cost. (3) The Rust skeleton in the design doc has ~30-45 lines of real logic (platform detect, backend select, hardcoded YAML template); runner.rs is all placeholders and service install 'not yet implemented' — 100% of operationally hard code deferred, and the design merely wraps NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner, a real existing tool that already implements Tart/libvirt/Docker per-job ephemeral runners, so no custom Rust binary is needed even for the pilot (a static outrunner.yml per host + bash install script suffices at 2 hosts). (4) The recommendation matches repo policy (CLAUDE.md 'Self-Hosted Runner Changes — must be in git and portable', install.sh as source of truth) and the existing monitor/alert/cleanup mesh (monitor.sh, slack-alert.sh, heal-runners.sh, cleanup scripts). Severity 'major' is proportionate since the finding rejects the doc's core deliverable with sound factual basis. Only cosmetic quibbles found (stale README runner count, 'rewrite' vs 'wrapper' wording), neither of which undermines the finding.
